name: Tadalafil-Nitroglycerin Time-Course Interaction Extract
population_or_context: 150 male subjects in a clinical pharmacology crossover study; direct for interaction pharmacology and indirect for broad healthy-user absolute risk.
methods: Subjects received seven consecutive daily doses of tadalafil 20 mg or placebo, then sublingual nitroglycerin 0.4 mg at defined intervals after the last dose; after washout, subjects crossed over.
population_profile: Male clinical pharmacology participants; relevant to men age 30 to 50 because the interaction is pharmacodynamic, but full age and comorbidity distribution was not extracted from full text.
tadalafil_exposure: Tadalafil 20 mg daily for seven consecutive days before repeated sublingual nitroglycerin challenges.
indication_context: Clinical pharmacology safety interaction study, not therapeutic off-label use.
study_design: Double-blind randomized placebo-controlled crossover multicenter study.
comparator_context: Placebo crossover comparison with nitroglycerin at 4, 8, 24, 48, 72, and 96 hours after final tadalafil/placebo dose.
safety_findings: Standing systolic blood pressure below 85 mm Hg and larger blood-pressure decreases were more frequent with tadalafil plus nitroglycerin at 4, 8, and 24 hours, but not 48 hours and beyond.
directness_to_target_population: High for nitrate co-use risk because the interaction is drug-mechanism based; not a direct estimate of real-world event rates in healthy off-label users.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Abstract-only extraction; controlled nitroglycerin challenges in monitored participants do not represent emergency nitrate use, alcohol co-use, dehydration, or unrecognized cardiovascular disease.
